title: Librarian Manifest
description: Combined manifest output from `python -m librarian manifest`. Contains three sub-manifests: portable registry snapshot, SHA-256 cryptographic hashes, and dependency graph.

generator_version.description: Librarian package version that produced this manifest.

repo_root.description: Absolute path to the project root at generation time.

registry_path.description: Absolute path to the REGISTRY.yaml consumed.

total_registered.description: Number of document entries in REGISTRY.yaml.

total_on_disk.description: Number of registered documents found on disk.

total_hashed.description: Number of files successfully hashed (equals total_on_disk when all files exist).

total_edges.description: Number of dependency edges in the graph.

registry_snapshot.description: Type 1: full REGISTRY.yaml content as JSON. Omitted (empty object) when --no-snapshot is passed.

file_hashes.description: Type 2: SHA-256 hash of every registered file. Empty array when --no-hashes is passed.

manifest_sha256.description: Tamper-evident seal: SHA-256 of the sorted 'filename:hash' lines for all existing files. Empty string when hashes are omitted.

dependency_edges.description: Type 3: cross-reference, supplement, and supersedes edges. Empty array when --no-graph is passed.

document_entry.description: A single document registration from REGISTRY.yaml.

file_hash.description: SHA-256 hash result for one registered file.

sha256.description: Hex-encoded SHA-256 hash. Empty string if file does not exist on disk.

size_bytes.description: File size in bytes. Zero if file does not exist.

exists.description: Whether the file was found on disk at generation time.

dependency_edge.description: One directional edge in the document dependency graph.

source.description: Filename of the document that declares the reference.

sections.description: Specific sections in the target document that the reference points to.

status.description: Edge status. 'resolved'/'pending'/'unknown' for cross-references; 'supplement' for supplements relationships; 'supersedes' for version chain edges.
